title: NPC Quest (Errand)
description: SNG-203 tier 6. The lightest tier — an errand. quest_structure.json's own rule: 'if you cannot name the cost of ignoring it, it is an errand, not a quest.' Errands are the texture of a living world; they are legitimate but must NOT be logged as, or held to the standard of, real quests. This schema DROPS the branched-outcome and multi-route requirements a real quest demands, and keeps only: giver, want, task (one testable objective), reward, and a single effect. An npc_quest that GROWS real stakes gets PROMOTED to quest_structure (the promotion path is a seam, SNG-203 §5). GM generates these freely via generate('npc_quest', ctx) from any NPC's want — this is the highest-volume, lowest-cost generation tier.

giver.description: npc id — the quest belongs to one person's want

want.description: the NPC's want, from their record — WHY they ask. Not a plot; a person needing a small thing.

task.description: ONE engine-testable objective. place reached / person spoken to / thing obtained / thing delivered. Single step — an errand with multiple gated stages is a quest; promote it.

reward.description: small and concrete — coin, an item, a fact, a favor, standing. Named, not vague.

effect.description: ONE machine-readable delta, usually a disposition bump or a codex fact. The errand's mark on the world is small by design.

promotable.description: true if this errand can reveal real stakes on completion and be promoted to a full quest_structure quest. The GM sets this when the want has a shadow (SNG-203 §5).

designLaws: An errand is not a quest and must not be logged as one. Naming it honestly is the point of a separate tier.
One step, one reward, one small mark. An errand that needs stages is a quest wearing errand's clothes — promote it, do not cram it.
The best errands make a person slightly warmer or a place slightly known. That is enough. Not everything must matter to the world.
Promotion is a gift, not a bait-and-switch: an errand that turns out to matter should feel like the world had depth, not like the errand lied.
